tasks.title: Fix TUI task-run viewer rendering and unblock phase-violation selection
evidence: User request: "The TUI viewer is busted when running a task. See the screenshots here and fix. ''/Users/mitchfultz/Screenshots/Screenshot 2026-01-22 at 11.13.15 AM.png'' and ''/Users/mitchfultz/Screenshots/Screenshot 2026-01-22 at 11.13.48 AM.png''. Secondly, when issues arise, and I am prompted "Phase X violations: revert uncommited changes" I am unable to select an option so it hangs forever. I MUST be able to select an option. Figure it out. View the screenshots, fix the UI, figure out a way to test the actual visuals so this doesn't happen again or regress."
plan: Reproduce the TUI task-run viewer rendering issue using the provided screenshots and identify the layout/viewport failure.
Fix the TUI rendering/layout bug so the task-run viewer is readable and stable across terminal sizes.
Fix input handling for the "Phase X violations: revert uncommited changes" prompt so selection always works and cannot hang.
Add automated visual or snapshot tests covering the task-run viewer and the phase-violation prompt to prevent regressions.
tasks.request: The TUI viewer is busted when running a task. See the screenshots here and fix. ''/Users/mitchfultz/Screenshots/Screenshot 2026-01-22 at 11.13.15 AM.png'' and ''/Users/mitchfultz/Screenshots/Screenshot 2026-01-22 at 11.13.48 AM.png''. Secondly, when issues arise, and I am prompted "Phase X violations: revert uncommited changes" I am unable to select an option so it hangs forever. I MUST be able to select an option. Figure it out. View the screenshots, fix the UI, figure out a way to test the actual visuals so this doesn't happen again or regress.

tasks.title: Add configurable CI gate command with opt-out
evidence: User request: "Currently all validation logic revolves around the 'make ci' gate. However, come projects do not have 'make ci'. We need to be able to allow customization of the CI gate command. Users can set the command to be valid based on the project, but it defaults to the current 'make ci'. If a user really wants to completely ignore any CI gate at all we should have a way to allow it."
plan: Inspect current CI gate execution and config flow to identify where the command is hard-coded.
Add a configurable CI gate command with default of `make ci`, and allow explicit opt-out/disable behavior.
Update CLI/help/docs to describe configuration and opt-out, and add tests covering default, custom, and disabled gate behavior.
tasks.request: Currently all validation logic revolves around the 'make ci' gate. However, come projects do not have 'make ci'. We need to be able to allow customization of the CI gate command. Users can set the command to be valid based on the project, but it defaults to the current 'make ci'. If a user really wants to completely ignore any CI gate at all we should have a way to allow it.

tasks.title: Add ralph scan support in the TUI
evidence: User request: "Add 'ralph scan' support to the ralph tui"
plan: Review current TUI entrypoints and command wiring to identify where scan actions should live.
Implement TUI flow to configure and trigger `ralph scan` from within the interface.
Add tests covering the new TUI scan action, including failure modes and validation.
tasks.request: Add 'ralph scan' support to the ralph tui

tasks.title: Add in-TUI help overlay for keybindings
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/render.rs` only shows a compact footer; there is no full help overlay or key to open it.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/events.rs` has no `?`/`h` handler to show help.
plan: Add a Help mode with a full-screen overlay listing keybindings and actions.
Wire `?`/`h` to toggle Help mode and update footer hints.
Add tests to ensure help mode transitions and rendering paths.

tasks.title: Add empty-queue action prompts (create task / open task command)
evidence: When no tasks exist, `crates/ralph/src/tui/render.rs` renders a static message and provides no action to create tasks in TUI.
plan: Add CTA hints in the empty state (e.g., `n` to create).
Ensure the keybinding launches the creation flow or defers to CLI guidance.
Add tests for empty-state rendering and key behavior.

tasks.title: Document TUI usage and keybindings in CLI docs
evidence: `docs/cli.md` documents `-i` but does not list TUI keybindings or behaviors; keybindings are only in the TUI module doc comment (`crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs`).
plan: Add a TUI section to `docs/cli.md` describing entrypoints and keybindings.
Keep docs aligned with actual keybindings and modes.
Add a documentation check in tests if applicable (or update existing doc tests).

tasks.title: Update `ralph run --help` to surface TUI entrypoints and examples
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs` `RunArgs` after_long_help examples do not include `-i` or TUI launch guidance, so `ralph run --help` omits interactive discovery.
plan: Add TUI examples and a short note to the `RunArgs` after_long_help string.
Verify `ralph run --help` output includes the interactive entrypoint.
Add a small CLI help snapshot test if one exists.

tasks.title: Add CLI parse tests for TUI entrypoint and interactive flags
evidence: Only a single interactive-related test exists (`cli_rejects_run_one_id_with_interactive`) in `crates/ralph/src/cli/mod.rs`; there are no tests ensuring valid `-i` paths parse correctly.
plan: Add CLI parsing tests for `ralph run one -i` and `ralph run loop -i`.
If a new TUI subcommand is added, add parse tests for it.
Ensure tests cover negative and positive interactive cases.
